name: Legal Framework Order 1970
era: Transition — From Martial Law to the 1973 Constitution (1969–1973)
key_provisions: One Unit dissolved — Punjab, Sindh, NWFP and Balochistan restored as provinces (1 July 1970)
Parity abolished: seats by population, giving East Pakistan 162 of 300 general seats
National Assembly to be a constituent body with a 120-day deadline, subject to the President's authentication
Elections held 7 December 1970: Awami League swept the East (160 seats), the Pakistan People's Party led the West (81)
demise: The Assembly it created never convened as scheduled; the postponement of March 1971 precipitated the final crisis.
